CONTENT.Honours: ArgentinaCopa América: 2021Superclásico de las Américas: 2019Argentine Primera División Best Midfielder: 2018–19Argentine Primera División Team of the Year: 2018–19
CONTENT.Club career: Domínguez played his first professional game for Vélez Sarsfield in a 3–2 victory against Estudiantes for the 15th fixture of the 2016–17 Argentine Primera División, under Omar De Felippe's coaching. He scored his first goal in a 3–0 victory against Tigre for the 28th fixture of that season.On 30 August 2019, he signed a contract with Italian club Bologna and was loaned back to Vélez Sarsfield for the rest of 2019. Domínguez made his debut for Bologna as a sub on 12 January in a 1–0 loss away at Torino. He would make his first start for the club on 19 January in a 1–1 home draw against Hellas Verona.On 1 September 2023, Domínguez signed with Premier League side Nottingham Forest on a five-year deal in swap deal for Remo Freuler. He made his debut away at Manchester City later that month. On 1 October, Domínguez scored his first goal for Nottingham Forest in a 1–1 draw against Brentford.

CONTENT.Career statistics: As of match played 11 May 2024As of match played 16 November 2021Scores and results list Argentina's goal tally first, score column indicates score after each Domínguez goal.
CONTENT.International career: Domínguez made his debut for Argentina national team on 5 September 2019 in a friendly against Chile, as a 67th-minute substitute for Rodrigo De Paul.

SUMMARY: Nicolás Martín Domínguez (Spanish pronunciation: [nikoˈlas ðoˈmiŋɡes]; born 28 June 1998) is an Argentine professional footballer who plays as a central midfielder for Premier League club Nottingham Forest.
